source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Management Server Optimizations: Caching facts and encrypting secrets with vault

questions.q: Gathering facts can be slow. What two ways does the chapter give to reduce the cost?
options: Disable it with gather_facts where unneeded, and/or cache the facts
questions.why: Use gather_facts: false when facts aren't needed, or cache facts for a period.

questions.q: Which datastore does the chapter use to cache facts?
questions.why: Facts are stored in a redis database (fact_caching = redis).

questions.q: Which ansible.cfg setting controls how long cached facts live?
Which tool encrypts secrets and variables in Ansible?
questions.why: ansible-vault encrypts a variable or a whole file.

questions.q: Encrypt the file myfile.yml with ansible-vault.
questions.why: ansible-vault encrypt <file> encrypts it in place.

questions.q: Edit an already-encrypted file myfile.yml with ansible-vault.
questions.why: ansible-vault edit decrypts the file for editing, then re-encrypts on save.

questions.q: An ansible-vault encrypted file can be recognized by which header?
questions.why: The file starts with the line $ANSIBLE_VAULT;1.1;AES256.

questions.q: Which ansible.cfg option points to the file holding the vault password (for non-interactive decryption)?
questions.why: vault_password_file = /etc/ansible/vault_pass lets Ansible decrypt at runtime.
